body: Bead ddx-a946c744 was decomposed into two child beads rather than implemented in one attempt. Size check failed: 10 ACs spanning a new cli/internal/checks/ package, integration into the 1693-line execute_bead_loop.go merge flow, a new bead schema field (checks_bypass) with event recording, a new ddx ac CLI command, and two e2e fixture-driven try-runs. That mixes design, implementation, integration, and e2e across unrelated subsystems and is well over the ~6-AC / ~500-line / ~5-file threshold. Decomposition: - ddx-aee651ec — checks protocol package (protocol.go, loader.go, runner.go, *_test.go) + ddx ac run CLI subcommand. Self-contained, unit-testable, no agent loop touch. - ddx-3e60fd84 — pre_merge hook integration in execute_bead_loop.go + checks_bypass bead annotation + event recording + two e2e fixture tests. Depends on ddx-aee651ec. Dependency edge recorded: ddx-3e60fd84 blocks-on ddx-aee651ec. Parent ddx-a946c744 updated with notes pointing at children. Once both children land, the parent's ACs are fully covered and the parent can be closed without additional implementation work. No source files in this worktree were modified.

no_changes
rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: Bead ddx-a946c744 was decomposed into two child beads rather than implemented in one attempt. Size check failed: 10 ACs spanning a new cli/internal/checks/ package, integration into the 1693-line execute_bead_loop.go merge flow, a new bead schema field (checks_bypass) with event recording, a new ddx ac CLI command, and two e2e fixture-driven try-runs. That mixes design, implementation, integration, and e2e across unrelated subsystems and is well over the ~6-AC / ~500-line / ~5-file threshold.

Decomposition:
- ddx-aee651ec — checks protocol package (protocol.go, loader.go, runner.go, *_test.go) + ddx ac run CLI subcommand. Self-contained, unit-testable, no agent loop touch.
- ddx-3e60fd84 — pre_merge hook integration in execute_bead_loop.go + checks_bypass bead annotation + event recording + two e2e fixture tests. Depends on ddx-aee651ec.

Dependency edge recorded: ddx-3e60fd84 blocks-on ddx-aee651ec. Parent ddx-a946c744 updated with notes pointing at children. Once both children land, the parent's ACs are fully covered and the parent can be closed without additional implementation work.

No source files in this worktree were modified.

retry_after=2026-05-03T02:51:35Z

Bead ddx-a946c744 — no changes this attempt.

What is done:
- Reviewed the bead description, acceptance criteria, and notes.

What is blocking:
- The bead's own <notes> field declares it has been decomposed:
  "decomposed into ddx-aee651ec (protocol package + ddx ac CLI) and
   ddx-3e60fd84 (pre_merge integration + checks_bypass + e2e).
   ddx-3e60fd84 depends on ddx-aee651ec."
- The full AC set (1-10) spans both child beads: protocol/loader/runner/ddx ac CLI
  belong to ddx-aee651ec; pre_merge integration, checks_bypass, and the
  end-to-end fixtures belong to ddx-3e60fd84.
- Implementing the parent here would duplicate or pre-empt the decomposed
  children, creating merge conflicts when those beads execute and violating the
  one-bead-one-commit boundary the queue relies on.

What a follow-up attempt would need:
- Either (a) close ddx-a946c744 administratively as superseded by its children
  (preferred — the decomposition note is the contract), or
- (b) if the parent should still be executed monolithically, remove the
  decomposition note and cancel/close the two child beads first so this attempt
  is not racing them.

Recommended action: close ddx-a946c744 as superseded; let ddx-aee651ec then
ddx-3e60fd84 execute in dependency order.
